name: Etruscan Bucchero

visual_attributes.morphology: vessel with thin or thick walls, often with handles
distinctive_features: thicker, squat proportions in bucchero pesante
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies from small cups to larger amphorae
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often includes handles and decorative incisions or mold-formed figures

domain_specific_visual.decoration: incised lines, toothed wheel patterns, mold-formed figures

domain_specific_functional.cultural_influence: influenced by Greek and Eastern Mediterranean pottery

relationships.description: Etruscan bucchero was influenced by Greek vase shapes and aesthetics.
Bucchero was produced in the Etruscan heartland of central Italy.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Etruscan heartland, including Caere, Veii, and Tarquinia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Regarded as the national pottery of ancient Etruria, symbolizing Etruscan craftsmanship and cultural identity.

contrastive_constraints: NOT red or tan colored pottery
